reasoning: The kernel performs 5 loads and 1 store of float32 per output element (24 bytes transferred) with only ~9 FLOPs (5 multiplies + 4 adds), yielding an arithmetic intensity of ~0.375 FLOP/byte — far below the A10G ridge point of 62 FLOP/byte. The access pattern across `a` and `b` is streaming with near-unit-stride, so DRAM bandwidth utilization will be high. With 4096 blocks × 256 threads = 1M threads processing 64M elements, there is ample parallelism to saturate memory bandwidth, confirming this is classically memory-bound rather than latency- or occupancy-limited.
